codeReview.summary: The fix correctly refactors the DealerCombobox clear action by replacing the nested `<button>` inside the outer combobox trigger with a `<span role="button" tabIndex={0}>` element. The implementation properly preserves all click behavior (with e.stopPropagation()), adds keyboard support for Enter/Space keys, and retains visual styling with cursor-pointer. A new test explicitly verifies the clear action element is not a native <button> to prevent regression. All verification steps (lint, typecheck, 1118 tests) passed. The DOM nesting warning is confirmed eliminated.

issues.description: The `<span role="button">` element lacks focus-visible styling. Native buttons get focus-visible:ring from .btn class in global.css, but custom span[role=button] elements don't have equivalent focus indication. This is a minor accessibility UX gap - the element is keyboard-accessible but doesn't show visible focus ring. Same pattern exists in SupportTicketsPage.tsx:275-277. Consider documenting or adding global CSS for `[role="button"]:focus-visible`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The pattern of using `<span role="button" tabIndex={0}>` with onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space keys to avoid nested-button DOM warnings is used in at least two places (CreateOrderDrawer.tsx:171-184, SupportTicketsPage.tsx:275-277). This could be documented as a project convention for clickable non-button elements inside buttons.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: CreateOrderDrawer.tsx:171-184 implements span[role=button] pattern. SupportTicketsPage.tsx:275-277 uses identical pattern. Both are for avoiding nested-button DOM structure while maintaining accessibility.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Global CSS defines focus-visible styles only for .btn class buttons, not for elements with role="button". This creates inconsistency where some interactive elements show focus ring and others don't.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: global.css:32 has `focus-visible:ring-2` for .btn class. No matching rule exists for `[role="button"]:focus-visible`. The new span[role=button] in CreateOrderDrawer.tsx:171 doesn't receive focus-visible styling.

summary: PASS. The fix correctly resolves the nested-button DOM nesting warning by replacing the inner `<button>` with a `<span role="button">` that maintains full click and keyboard accessibility. All tests pass (1118 total), lint and typecheck pass. Minor non-blocking observation about missing focus-visible styling for role=button elements, which is consistent with an existing pattern in the codebase.
